tips.text: You have a built-in journaling system. Say "let's journal" or use /journaling-assistant to start a conversational daily entry that gets saved as a dated Markdown file.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user is reflecting on their day, venting, describing events, or seems to want to record something.

tips.text: Type /toolkit to see all your installed features and useful phrases at a glance.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user seems unsure what the toolkit can do or asks about available features.

tips.text: Your toolkit can read and write files on Google Drive. Say "upload this to Drive" or use /google-drive for file operations.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user mentions files, documents, backups, or cloud storage.

tips.text: If something feels off, type /health to run a quick diagnostic that checks all your toolkit components.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user encounters errors, missing features, or something not working as expected.

tips.text: You can capture quick notes from your phone to a Todoist inbox. Claude will process them at the start of each session — answering questions, creating tasks, or filing information.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user mentions notes, quick capture, phone notes, or wants to remember something for later.

tips.text: Fork File tracks your groceries, pantry inventory, and fast food spending. Say "I bought groceries" or "log my fast food" to get started.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user mentions food, groceries, cooking, pantry, fridge, restaurants, or food spending.

tips.text: Type /update to check for and install the latest toolkit version. New features and fixes are released regularly.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user asks about new features, or if the statusline shows an update is available.

tips.text: Your journal entries feed into the Encyclopedia system — a set of modular files that build up a comprehensive record of your life, beliefs, goals, and relationships over time.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user has been journaling and mentions wanting to organize or look back at their life information.

tips.text: Use /encyclopedia-compile to generate a full biography document from your Encyclopedia source files. It weaves everything into a single readable narrative.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user wants a comprehensive view of their life record or mentions compiling their information.

tips.text: Need to look something up from your journal or Encyclopedia? The Librarian can search your entire life record — say "search my encyclopedia for..." or "brief me on [person]."
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user is trying to recall information about people, events, or past decisions they may have recorded.

tips.text: The Encyclopedia Interviewer proactively identifies gaps in your life record and runs focused interview sessions to fill them. Say "interview me" to start.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user has a mature Encyclopedia and mentions wanting to flesh out their record or fill gaps.

tips.text: Want to teach Claude a new trick? The Skill Creator helps you build custom skills — reusable capabilities that Claude can invoke. Say "create a skill" to get started.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user has a repetitive workflow they want to automate or mentions wanting Claude to do something it doesn't currently do.

tips.text: Your toolkit can sync memory, config, and CLAUDE.md across devices via Google Drive or GitHub. Check /health to see your sync status.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user mentions multiple devices, syncing, or backing up their Claude configuration.

tips.text: At the end of each journaling session, your entries are automatically routed to the right Encyclopedia source files — people go to People Database, events to Chronicle, goals to Open Threads.
tips.context_hint: Suggest after a journaling session or when the user asks how journal entries are organized long-term.

tips.text: Built something cool with the toolkit? Type /contribute to package your improvements and share them back with other DestinClaude users.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user has created custom skills or made modifications they're proud of.

tips.text: Claude remembers things across conversations using a file-based memory system. Say "remember that I prefer..." to save preferences, or "what do you remember about..." to recall.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user corrects Claude's behavior, states a preference, or asks if Claude remembers something.

tips.text: You can re-run /setup-wizard at any time to change your comfort level, reconfigure integrations, or set up features you skipped the first time.
tips.context_hint: Suggest when the user wants to change settings, add an integration they skipped, or adjust their comfort level.
